description: Forensic animation quality baseline — validated camera, lighting, and material settings for EEVEE renders

engine.name: BLENDER_EEVEE
engine.note: Blender 5.1 renamed EEVEE_NEXT back to BLENDER_EEVEE. Use version check: bpy.app.version >= (5,0,0)

color_management.note: AgX provides 25-stop dynamic range. May score slightly lower on Tier 1 pixel scorer but looks better visually.

camera_positions.note: CRITICAL: Camera distance was #1 quality killer. Scene 3 cameras at 480m scored 33; at 40m scored 70.

BirdEye.rotation: straight down (-90 deg X or (0,0,0) euler)

DriverPOV.note: Must add cabin lighting if inside vehicle

SecurityCam.note: Wide angle security camera lens, tilted 30-45 deg down

day_scenes.note: EEVEE SUN at 5W is correct brightness. Do NOT use Cycles values (need 3-4x more).

night_scenes.note: Night needs 3+ area fill lights with warm sodium color. World strength 0.3 (not 0.05).

materials.note: Material name matching is fragile. Always audit object names per scene before assigning.

forensic_overlays.evidence_markers: Emissive cones (R/B/Y), radius 0.3m, height 0.8m, emission strength 3.0
forensic_overlays.exhibit_label: 3D text on ground plane, case number + disclaimer

critical_rules: NEVER switch from EEVEE to Cycles without adjusting light energy 3-4x
NEVER place cameras more than 60m from scene center
NEVER use world background strength < 0.1 for night scenes
ALWAYS audit camera positions before rendering (lsof check is not enough)
ALWAYS use --background flag for headless Blender (never open GUI from scheduled tasks)
ALWAYS check bpy.app.version for engine name selection
CLI rendering (--background --python) is more reliable than MCP for batch ops
